channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Rockefeller Foundation Managing Director, National Partnerships Job: Lead Large-Scale Impact in 2026
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Rockefeller Foundation Managing Director, National Partnerships job offers a high-impact leadership opportunity for experienced professionals looking to shape national systems and scale economic opportunity initiatives. Based in New York (with an option for Washington, DC), this senior-level role focuses on building strategic partnerships, aligning funding, and translating local innovation into national impact. About the […]

The post Rockefeller Foundation Managing Director, National Partnerships Job: Lead Large-Scale Impact in 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Rockefeller Foundation Managing Director, National Partnerships job offers a high-impact leadership opportunity for experienced professionals looking to shape national systems and scale economic opportunity initiatives. Based in New York (with an option for Washington, DC), this senior-level role focuses on building strategic partnerships, aligning funding, and translating local innovation into national impact.

About the Program

This role is offered by The Rockefeller Foundation, a globally recognized philanthropy committed to advancing human well-being through innovation in areas such as health, food systems, energy, and economic opportunity.

The Managing Director, National Partnerships plays a central role in advancing the Foundation’s U.S. Economic Opportunity and Civic Innovation agenda. Reporting to senior leadership, the role focuses on scaling proven solutions by connecting community-based insights with national platforms, policies, and funding strategies.

The position is highly external-facing and involves working closely with stakeholders across sectors—including philanthropy, government, financial institutions, and employers—to build partnerships that drive long-term, systemic change.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Addressing economic inequality and workforce challenges requires more than isolated interventions—it demands coordinated, large-scale solutions. This role is designed to bridge that gap by ensuring that successful local initiatives are translated into national systems and policies.

By aligning funding streams, influencing institutional practices, and fostering cross-sector collaboration, the Managing Director contributes to building sustainable pathways for economic mobility. The role also positions the successful candidate as a key influencer in shaping national conversations around workforce development and inclusive economic growth.

For professionals seeking to operate at the intersection of policy, finance, and social impact, this opportunity offers a platform to drive meaningful, large-scale change.

What Participants Will Gain

This position provides significant leadership experience at a national level, offering the opportunity to design and implement strategies that scale impact across multiple states and sectors.

The selected candidate will strengthen their expertise in partnership development, managing complex stakeholder ecosystems, and structuring funding strategies that combine philanthropic, public, and private capital.

The role also offers high-level exposure to policy development and institutional change, allowing professionals to influence how economic opportunity initiatives are designed and implemented across the United States.

In addition, working with the Rockefeller Foundation provides access to a global network of leaders, innovators, and institutions, enhancing both professional credibility and long-term career prospects.

Who Can Apply

This role is intended for senior professionals with extensive leadership experience. Applicants are generally expected to have at least 12 years of experience in fields such as philanthropy, government, finance, or economic development.

Candidates should demonstrate a strong track record of building and managing multi-stakeholder partnerships, as well as experience in scaling programs or initiatives to achieve broader impact.

A bachelor’s degree is required, while an advanced degree is preferred. Strong strategic thinking, leadership, and communication skills are essential, along with the ability to navigate complex systems and influence high-level stakeholders.

This position is based in New York City, United States, with an additional location option in Washington, DC. The role follows a hybrid work schedule, requiring in-office presence Monday through Thursday.

Application Process and Deadline

Interested candidates should apply through the official Rockefeller Foundation careers page, ensuring all required materials are submitted before the deadline.

The application deadline is April 29, 2026. Due to the senior nature of this role, applicants are encouraged to submit well-prepared applications highlighting leadership experience, partnership-building, and impact at scale.

Learn more and apply on the official Rockefeller Foundation website to be considered for this leadership role shaping national impact. When applying, highlight your experience scaling initiatives, managing partnerships, and influencing policy or funding systems.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Rockefeller Foundation Managing Director, National Partnerships Job: Lead Large-Scale Impact in 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNFPA ICT Associate Job in Ghana (G7): Build a Career in Global Development and Technology
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The UNFPA ICT Associate Job in Ghana (G7) offers a valuable opportunity for technology professionals to apply their skills within an international development context while contributing to impactful global initiatives. Based in Accra, this full-time position allows candidates to support digital operations and strengthen ICT systems within one of the United Nations’ leading agencies. About […]

The post UNFPA ICT Associate Job in Ghana (G7): Build a Career in Global Development and Technology appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The UNFPA ICT Associate Job in Ghana (G7) offers a valuable opportunity for technology professionals to apply their skills within an international development context while contributing to impactful global initiatives. Based in Accra, this full-time position allows candidates to support digital operations and strengthen ICT systems within one of the United Nations’ leading agencies.

About the Program

The ICT Associate role is offered by United Nations Population Fund, the UN agency dedicated to ensuring that every pregnancy is intended, every childbirth is safe, and every young person can reach their full potential. Operating across multiple countries, UNFPA plays a key role in advancing global development goals, particularly in areas such as reproductive health, gender equality, and population data.

In Ghana, the ICT Associate will work within a dynamic country office environment, supporting the implementation of digital systems and infrastructure. The role is supervised by the International Operations Manager and involves overseeing ICT services, ensuring system reliability, and providing technical support to staff.

This is a fixed-term position with an initial duration of one year, with the possibility of extension based on performance and organizational needs.

Why This Opportunity Matters

As organizations increasingly rely on digital infrastructure, ICT professionals play a critical role in ensuring operational efficiency and service delivery. Within UNFPA, this role goes beyond technical support—it directly contributes to programs that improve lives and strengthen communities.

By maintaining secure and reliable systems, the ICT Associate enables teams to deliver essential services related to healthcare, gender equality, and development. This position therefore combines technical expertise with social impact, making it particularly relevant for professionals seeking purpose-driven careers.

Additionally, working within a UN agency provides exposure to international standards, cross-cultural collaboration, and complex operational environments, all of which are valuable for long-term career growth.

What Participants Will Gain

The ICT Associate will gain extensive experience managing and supporting enterprise-level ICT systems within a global organization. This includes working with infrastructure, software systems, and security protocols that support large-scale operations.

The role also offers the opportunity to develop leadership skills by overseeing ICT functions within the country office. Candidates will enhance their ability to troubleshoot technical issues, manage vendors, and implement digital strategies that improve organizational efficiency.

Beyond technical growth, the position provides exposure to international development work, allowing professionals to understand how technology supports global initiatives and policy implementation.

Working within UNFPA also enables candidates to build a strong professional network, connecting with experts across different sectors and countries.

Who Can Apply

This position is open to candidates with a bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field. Applicants should have at least three years of relevant professional experience in ICT roles within either the public or private sector.

Candidates should demonstrate strong technical knowledge, including familiarity with operating systems, network infrastructure, and ICT security systems. Experience with enterprise resource planning systems and corporate ICT environments is considered an advantage.

Strong analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills are essential, along with the ability to manage multiple tasks and respond to technical challenges effectively.

Fluency in English is required, and knowledge of an additional UN language may strengthen an application.

Eligible Areas

This is a national position based in Accra, Ghana. Applicants must be eligible to work in Ghana.

Application Process and Deadline

The application deadline is May 9, 2026. Due to the competitive nature of UN roles, early applications are strongly encouraged.

Shortlisted candidates may be required to undergo additional assessments, including technical evaluations and interviews.

Learn more and apply on the official UNFPA job portal.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post UNFPA ICT Associate Job in Ghana (G7): Build a Career in Global Development and Technology appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Youth Leadership Academy Cambodia 2026: Build Feminist Leadership Skills and Drive Social Change
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Youth Leadership Academy (YLA) Cambodia 2026 is a year-long leadership programme designed to empower young women with the skills, confidence, and networks needed to challenge gender inequality and create meaningful change in their communities. Supported by UN Women Cambodia, this initiative provides a safe and inclusive space for emerging leaders to develop transformative leadership […]

The post Youth Leadership Academy Cambodia 2026: Build Feminist Leadership Skills and Drive Social Change appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Youth Leadership Academy (YLA) Cambodia 2026 is a year-long leadership programme designed to empower young women with the skills, confidence, and networks needed to challenge gender inequality and create meaningful change in their communities. Supported by UN Women Cambodia, this initiative provides a safe and inclusive space for emerging leaders to develop transformative leadership and critical thinking abilities.

About the Program

The Youth Leadership Academy (YLA) is a unique programme co-created by Cambodian women leaders from diverse backgrounds who are passionate about advancing gender equality. It is rooted in lived experiences and designed to respond to the real challenges young women face in accessing leadership opportunities.

The programme runs over 8–10 months and combines four in-person workshops with mentorship and action-based learning. Participants engage in a structured journey that emphasizes personal growth, collaborative learning, and real-world application of feminist leadership principles.

Through this blended approach, participants are not only introduced to leadership concepts but are also supported in applying them in their daily lives, families, and communities. The programme prioritizes creating a safe, supportive environment where young women can express themselves, build confidence, and develop their voices.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Across many communities, young women—particularly those from marginalized backgrounds—face barriers to leadership development, networking opportunities, and access to platforms where their voices can be heard. The Youth Leadership Academy addresses these challenges by intentionally focusing on inclusion and equity.

By centering feminist leadership, the programme encourages participants to question societal norms, reflect critically on gender dynamics, and take action to address inequalities. This is particularly important in contexts where discussions around gender and social justice may be limited or inaccessible.

The programme also emphasizes community impact. Rather than focusing solely on individual development, YLA supports participants in becoming change-makers who can influence their environments and contribute to broader social transformation.

What Participants Will Gain

Participants in the Youth Leadership Academy gain a wide range of personal and professional benefits. They develop strong leadership and critical thinking skills that enable them to navigate complex social issues and advocate for change.

The programme helps participants build confidence and communication skills, empowering them to express their ideas clearly and engage in meaningful discussions. Through mentorship and peer learning, participants also expand their networks, connecting with like-minded individuals and experienced leaders.

A key component of the programme is action learning, where participants apply their knowledge to real-life situations. This hands-on approach strengthens their ability to translate ideas into practical solutions and initiatives within their communities.

Additionally, participants gain a deeper understanding of gender equality and feminist leadership, equipping them with the tools to challenge inequality and promote inclusive practices in various aspects of their lives.

Who Can Apply

The programme is open to young women aged 17 to 25 years old who are passionate about gender equality and social change. It is particularly aimed at individuals who have had limited access to leadership programmes or networks.

Young women from underrepresented groups, including those with disabilities or from ethnic minority communities, are strongly encouraged to apply. Applicants should demonstrate a genuine interest in issues affecting women and girls, as well as a willingness to engage in feminist leadership practices.

Participants must be available to fully commit to the programme over its duration, including attending all workshops and actively participating in mentorship and action learning activities.

Eligible Areas

This opportunity is specifically designed for young women based in Cambodia.

Application Process and Deadline

Applicants are required to complete an online application form before the deadline. The selection process focuses on identifying motivated individuals who show potential for leadership and a commitment to social impact.

The application deadline is April 30, 2026. Selected participants will be announced on May 8, 2026, followed by the programme’s inception workshop on May 17, 2026. The programme officially begins on May 22, 2026.

An online information session will also be held on April 18, 2026 to provide further details about the programme and application process.

Apply using the official Google Form application.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Youth Leadership Academy Cambodia 2026: Build Feminist Leadership Skills and Drive Social Change appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Call for Proposals: Sub-Grants to Scale Gender-Responsive Teacher Professional Development in Africa
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Education systems across Sub-Saharan Africa continue to face deep-rooted gender inequalities that limit learners’ full potential. To address this, VVOB – education for development, through the Regional Teachers Initiative for Africa (RTIA), has launched an exciting Call for Proposals aimed at strengthening and scaling impactful Teacher Professional Development (TPD) initiatives focused on Gender-Responsive Pedagogy (GRP). […]

The post Call for Proposals: Sub-Grants to Scale Gender-Responsive Teacher Professional Development in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Education systems across Sub-Saharan Africa continue to face deep-rooted gender inequalities that limit learners’ full potential. To address this, VVOB – education for development, through the Regional Teachers Initiative for Africa (RTIA), has launched an exciting Call for Proposals aimed at strengthening and scaling impactful Teacher Professional Development (TPD) initiatives focused on Gender-Responsive Pedagogy (GRP).

This funding opportunity is designed for organizations that have already piloted promising initiatives and are now ready to expand their impact sustainably within education systems.

About the Call for Proposals

This initiative supports organizations working to improve teaching practices by integrating gender-responsive approaches—especially at:

  • Early Childhood Education (ECE)
  • Lower Secondary Education

The goal is to move beyond small-scale pilot projects and support scalable, system-wide solutions that address gender norms as a root cause of inequality in education.

Objectives of the Sub-Grants

The Call for Proposals is structured around two major objectives:

1. Strengthening Scalability of GRP-Focused TPD Programs

Selected organizations will receive support to:

  • Address key barriers limiting their ability to scale
  • Strengthen their evidence base
  • Improve stakeholder alignment
  • Enhance operational capacity
  • Position their programs for integration into national education systems

2. Building Knowledge Across Sub-Saharan Africa

The initiative also aims to:

  • Generate practical insights on scaling teacher development programs
  • Share learnings across a community of practice
  • Contribute to a growing evidence base on gender norms transformation in education

What Kind of Projects Are Expected?

Applicants must propose targeted and practical interventions that:

  • Address one clearly defined constraint limiting program scalability
  • Align with a broader scaling strategy
  • Demonstrate measurable progress toward expansion and sustainability

Key Requirements:

  • Activities must be realistic and achievable within:
    • Funding limit: Up to EUR 40,000
    • Duration: Up to 12 months
  • Clear explanation of how the intervention will unlock scaling potential

Important Dates

  • Application Deadline: 29 May 2026
  • Evaluation & Notification: June 2026
  • Project Start Date: July 2026

How to Apply

Click Here for more and to apply

Why You Should Apply

This is more than just funding—it’s a strategic opportunity to:

  • Scale impactful education solutions across Africa
  • Address gender inequality at its root
  • Strengthen your organization’s capacity and influence
  • Contribute to system-level educational transformation
  • Join a network of organizations shaping the future of education

If your organization is already implementing a promising Teacher Professional Development initiative and is ready to scale, this call provides the resources, structure, and platform to take your impact to the next level.

For more grant opportunities click Here.

The post Call for Proposals: Sub-Grants to Scale Gender-Responsive Teacher Professional Development in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Commonwealth Secretariat Communications Internships 2026: Gain Paid Experience in Global Communications in London
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Commonwealth Secretariat Communications Internships 2026 offer early-career professionals a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience in global communications, digital media, and international development. Based in London, these six-month internships provide practical training, professional exposure, and the chance to contribute to high-level communications work across a network of 56 member countries. About the Program The […]

The post Commonwealth Secretariat Communications Internships 2026: Gain Paid Experience in Global Communications in London appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Commonwealth Secretariat Communications Internships 2026 offer early-career professionals a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience in global communications, digital media, and international development. Based in London, these six-month internships provide practical training, professional exposure, and the chance to contribute to high-level communications work across a network of 56 member countries.

About the Program

The Commonwealth Secretariat serves as the principal intergovernmental body supporting cooperation among its 56 member states, working to advance democracy, development, and respect for diversity. Its Communications Division plays a central role in promoting these values through strategic storytelling, media engagement, and digital content.

For 2026, the Secretariat is offering multiple internship opportunities within its Communications team, including a Communications Intern role and a Digital Communications Intern position. Both roles are designed to equip participants with practical skills in communications while contributing to impactful projects at an international level.

The internships run for six months and are based at Marlborough House in London. Interns will work closely with experienced professionals, supporting day-to-day communications activities while gaining exposure to global campaigns and initiatives, including preparations for major international events such as the Commonwealth Heads of Government Meeting.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Breaking into the communications field—particularly within international organizations—can be challenging without relevant experience. These internships address that gap by offering structured, hands-on learning within a globally recognized institution.

Participants gain insight into how international organizations communicate complex issues such as development, governance, and global cooperation. The experience also provides exposure to multicultural working environments, helping interns build cross-cultural communication skills that are highly valued in global careers.

Additionally, working within the Commonwealth Secretariat allows interns to contribute to meaningful initiatives that impact millions of people across member countries, making this more than just a professional opportunity—it is also a chance to support global development goals.

What Participants Will Gain

Interns will develop a broad set of practical skills that are directly applicable to careers in communications, media, and international development. They will gain experience in research, writing, and content creation, producing materials that are both informative and engaging.

Those in the Digital Communications role will build technical expertise in multimedia production, including photography, video editing, and graphic design. They will also gain hands-on experience managing digital platforms, creating content for websites and social media, and supporting online campaigns.

Across both roles, interns will strengthen their project management abilities by contributing to multiple initiatives simultaneously, including event planning and reporting. They will also learn how to analyze audience engagement and improve communication strategies based on data insights.

Equally important, participants will expand their professional networks by working with experienced communications professionals and engaging with stakeholders across different countries and sectors.

Successful candidates will be offered a structured internship experience offering a weekly stipend of £532.80.

Who Can Apply

The internships are open to early-career professionals and recent graduates in communications, journalism, or related fields. Candidates with up to two years of experience in communications, media, or public information roles are encouraged to apply.

Applicants should demonstrate strong writing, research, and organizational skills, along with attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple tasks effectively. Familiarity with digital tools such as content management systems, design software, and social media platforms is an advantage, particularly for the Digital Communications role.

For the Digital Communications Intern position, applicants should also have practical skills in content creation, including photography, video production, and graphic design. Interns must be under the age of 30 at the time of application.

An understanding of international development issues and experience working in or with developing countries can further strengthen an application.

Eligible Areas

These internships are open to candidates from across Commonwealth countries and beyond. However, selected candidates must be able to work in London for the duration of the six-month program.

Application Process and Deadline

Interested applicants must submit their applications through the official Commonwealth Secretariat careers portal. The selection process typically involves a review of qualifications, skills, and relevant experience.

While specific deadlines may vary depending on the role, applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible due to the competitive nature of these internships.

Apply for the Digital Communications Internship

Apply for the Communications Internship

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Commonwealth Secretariat Communications Internships 2026: Gain Paid Experience in Global Communications in London appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: African Academy for Women in Political Leadership 2026: Fully Funded UNDP Programme Empowering Africa’s Next Generation of Women Leaders
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The African Academy for Women in Political Leadership 2026 is a groundbreaking continental initiative designed to transform the landscape of political leadership across Africa by empowering women to take on influential governance roles. Spearheaded by the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) in partnership with the African School of Governance (ASG), the African Union Commission (AUC), […]

The post African Academy for Women in Political Leadership 2026: Fully Funded UNDP Programme Empowering Africa’s Next Generation of Women Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The African Academy for Women in Political Leadership 2026 is a groundbreaking continental initiative designed to transform the landscape of political leadership across Africa by empowering women to take on influential governance roles. Spearheaded by the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) in partnership with the African School of Governance (ASG), the African Union Commission (AUC), and the African Women Leaders Network (AWLN), this fully funded leadership programme aims to equip women with the tools, networks, and strategic capabilities needed to lead effectively at national and continental levels.

This prestigious programme represents a major milestone in advancing gender equality in governance and addressing the persistent underrepresentation of women in political leadership across Africa.

About the African Academy for Women in Political Leadership

The African Academy for Women in Political Leadership is a high-impact leadership development initiative created to build a strong pipeline of women leaders capable of shaping governance systems across Africa.

The programme is:

  • Anchored in African leadership and ownership
  • Designed as a continental public good
  • Built through a collaborative co-design process involving key stakeholders across Africa
  • Focused on delivering practical, strategic, and transformational leadership training

The Academy seeks to dismantle systemic barriers that limit women’s participation in politics and governance, while providing a platform for women to thrive and lead.

Why This Programme Matters

Despite progress in gender equality, women remain significantly underrepresented in political leadership across Africa. Many women who enter political spaces face structural challenges, limited access to networks, and systemic biases.

This programme directly addresses these challenges by:

  • Building leadership capacity among women
  • Providing access to high-level mentorship and networks
  • Enhancing strategic political skills
  • Promoting inclusive and ethical governance
  • Strengthening women’s participation in electoral and governance processes

The Academy is designed not just to train participants but to transform political leadership ecosystems across the continent.

Programme Structure and Format

The African Academy for Women in Political Leadership offers a four-week blended learning experience, combining virtual and in-person engagement.

Key Components of the Programme

  1. Virtual Learning Phase
    • Interactive sessions led by experts
    • Focus on theoretical and strategic frameworks
    • Engagement with peers across Africa
  2. Residential Convening in Kigali, Rwanda
    • Intensive, immersive leadership experience
    • In-person networking and collaboration
    • Engagement with senior African leaders
  3. Mentorship and Coaching
    • Direct mentorship from experienced political leaders
    • Guidance on navigating political systems
  4. Applied Leadership Practice
    • Practical exercises and real-world simulations
    • Leadership application in political contexts

Curriculum Focus Areas

Participants will gain in-depth knowledge and practical skills in the following areas:

  • Political leadership and governance
  • Strategic communications
  • Coalition-building and legislative processes
  • Campaign strategy and political financing
  • Ethical leadership and inclusive governance

These modules are designed to prepare participants for high-level political engagement and leadership responsibilities.

Fully Funded Opportunity

One of the most attractive aspects of this programme is that it is fully funded.

Covered Benefits Include:

  • Full tuition for the programme
  • Travel costs to Kigali
  • Accommodation during the residential convening
  • Participation in all programme activities
  • Access to mentorship and leadership networks

This ensures that financial barriers do not prevent qualified women from participating.

Who Should Apply

The programme targets women across Africa who are actively engaged in politics or aspiring to enter political leadership.

Eligible Categories of Applicants

1. Electoral Cycle Leaders

  • Women preparing for upcoming elections within 12–36 months
  • Parliamentary or presidential candidates
  • Campaign strategists
  • Members of Parliament seeking re-election or higher office

2. Sitting Political Leaders

  • Cabinet Ministers
  • Members of Parliament or Senate
  • Speakers and Deputy Speakers
  • Mayors and Governors
  • Senior political party leaders

3. Young Women Leaders (Aged 21–35)

  • Emerging leaders in politics or public institutions
  • Aspiring candidates at local or national levels
  • Women with demonstrated leadership potential

Special consideration is given to:

  • Women with disabilities
  • Women from fragile or transitional political environments

Baseline Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be a female citizen of an African Union Member State
  • Be at least 21 years old
  • Demonstrate active engagement in political leadership or ambition
  • Provide formal endorsement from:
    • A government institution,
    • A recognized political party, or
    • A relevant political authority
  • Commit to full participation in the programme and alumni activities

Selection Process

The selection process is:

  • Highly competitive
  • Merit-based
  • Regionally balanced

Selection Criteria Includes:

  • Leadership potential and impact
  • Political engagement
  • Regional and linguistic diversity
  • Gender inclusivity and representation

Cohort Composition Targets:

  • Maximum of 30 participants
  • Representation from all five African regions
  • Maximum of two participants per country
  • At least 60% from countries with upcoming elections
  • At least 40% young women (21–35)
  • Inclusion across:
    • Anglophone
    • Francophone
    • Lusophone
    • Arabophone Africa

Application Process

Applications are submitted through a nomination-based system.

SUBMIT YOUR APPLICATION

Who Can Nominate:

  • Government institutions
  • Political parties
  • African Union member state bodies
  • Civil society organizations

Self-nominations are allowed but must include institutional endorsement.

Required Documents:

  1. Official nomination or endorsement letter
  2. Curriculum Vitae (maximum 2 pages)
  3. Personal statement (maximum 1,000 words)

Application Deadline

Deadline: May 15, 2026

Late or incomplete applications will not be considered.

Conclusion

The African Academy for Women in Political Leadership 2026 is a transformative initiative aimed at reshaping governance across Africa by investing in women leaders. With its fully funded structure, high-level mentorship, and strategic curriculum, the programme offers an unparalleled opportunity for women to rise as influential political actors.

This is more than a leadership programme—it is a movement toward inclusive governance, gender equality, and sustainable political transformation in Africa.

Click Here for more information about the African Academy for Women in Political Leadership 2026.

Discover more global Fellowship, African, Women, and Leadership Development opportunities on Opportunities For Youth (OFY).

The post African Academy for Women in Political Leadership 2026: Fully Funded UNDP Programme Empowering Africa’s Next Generation of Women Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: World Economic Forum Internship 2026 in Beijing: Government, Business & Event Experience Roles for Global Impact Careers
item.dc:creator: Barbara Boampong
item.description:
The World Economic Forum (WEF) Internship Programme 2026 in Beijing offers an exceptional opportunity for students and early-career professionals to gain hands-on experience within one of the world’s most influential international organizations. Through multiple internship tracks—including Government Engagement, Business Engagement, and Media, Communities & Event Experience—this programme provides direct exposure to global policymaking, stakeholder engagement, […]

The post World Economic Forum Internship 2026 in Beijing: Government, Business & Event Experience Roles for Global Impact Careers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The World Economic Forum (WEF) Internship Programme 2026 in Beijing offers an exceptional opportunity for students and early-career professionals to gain hands-on experience within one of the world’s most influential international organizations. Through multiple internship tracks—including Government Engagement, Business Engagement, and Media, Communities & Event Experience—this programme provides direct exposure to global policymaking, stakeholder engagement, and high-level international collaboration.

These internships are designed for individuals who are passionate about global affairs, international cooperation, economic development, and diplomacy, and who are eager to contribute to initiatives that shape the future of the world.

About the World Economic Forum

The World Economic Forum is an independent international organization committed to improving the state of the world by fostering public-private cooperation. With over 55 years of experience, the Forum brings together leaders from government, business, academia, and civil society to address global challenges and create sustainable solutions.

Working at the Forum means contributing to:

  • Global dialogue and cooperation
  • Policy development and strategic initiatives
  • International partnerships and engagement
  • Innovative solutions to complex global issues

Overview of Internship Opportunities

The WEF Beijing office is offering three major internship tracks, each providing unique responsibilities and exposure:

  1. Government Engagement Internship
  2. Business Engagement Internship
  3. Media, Communities & Event Experience Internship

All internships are:

  • Full-time roles
  • Based in Beijing, China
  • Duration: 5–6 months
  • Start date: As soon as possible (rolling intake)

1. Government Engagement Internship

Team Overview

The Centre for Regions, Trade and Geopolitics (CRTG) focuses on engaging with high-level government officials and addressing key issues related to:

  • Regional development
  • International trade
  • Geopolitical dynamics

Key Responsibilities

  • Conduct data entry and maintain internal systems
  • Map roles of Chinese public figures and align them with Forum sessions
  • Translate documents (English ↔ Chinese)
  • Support liaison between Forum staff and government stakeholders
  • Provide logistics support for officials (travel, accommodation, meetings)
  • Draft official communications and request letters
  • Conduct policy research at national and local levels
  • Support high-level visits and events

Skills & Requirements

  • Strong understanding of Chinese government systems
  • Fluency in English and Chinese
  • Strong writing and communication skills
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office
  • Ability to multitask and work under pressure
  • Research and analytical skills

Learn more and apply Here

2. Business Engagement Internship

Team Overview

The China Business Engagement Group manages relationships with business partners and supports their involvement in Forum initiatives such as the Annual Meeting of the New Champions (AMNC) 2026.

Key Responsibilities

  • Support partner engagement strategies and internal reporting
  • Conduct industry research and prepare insights
  • Develop communication and promotional materials
  • Prepare meeting documents (agendas, reports, participant lists)
  • Coordinate speaker outreach and logistics for events
  • Maintain partner databases (e.g., Salesforce)
  • Assist with scheduling and operational coordination

Skills & Requirements

  • Background in economics, management, or related fields
  • Strong communication skills in English and Chinese
  • Proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint
  • Analytical thinking and research skills
  • Attention to detail and multitasking ability
  • Interest in global economic trends

Learn more and apply Here

3. Media, Communities & Event Experience Internship

Team Overview

This team plays a central role in organizing events and enhancing stakeholder engagement experiences across the Forum’s activities in Greater China.

Key Responsibilities

  • Analyze participant feedback and improve event experiences
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design solutions
  • Support digital tools and platforms for engagement
  • Assist in stakeholder communication and coordination
  • Improve event processes and participant journeys
  • Support logistics such as invitations, registration, and communications
  • Contribute to strategic projects post-AMNC

Skills & Requirements

  • Background in international relations, event management, or related fields
  • Strong organizational and time management skills
  • Fluency in English and Chinese
  • Problem-solving and analytical skills
  • High attention to detail and professionalism
  • Ability to work collaboratively across teams

Learn more and apply Here

Benefits of the Internship

Although compensation details are not explicitly stated, the WEF internship provides significant professional advantages:

  • Exposure to global policy and international relations
  • Direct experience working with government and business leaders
  • Participation in major global events like AMNC
  • Opportunity to build a global professional network
  • Development of high-demand skills in research, communication, and project management
  • Experience in a multicultural and high-impact environment

Why You Should Apply

The World Economic Forum internship is ideal for individuals who:

  • Want to work at the intersection of global policy and business
  • Are interested in diplomacy, governance, and international cooperation
  • Seek hands-on experience in a prestigious global organization
  • Aim to build a career in international development or global leadership

Application Process

Applicants are required to:

  • Submit their application through the WEF Careers Portal
  • Meet the qualifications specific to each role
  • Demonstrate language proficiency (English & Chinese)
  • Showcase relevant academic or professional experience

Application Deadline

No fixed deadline is explicitly stated. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and candidates are encouraged to apply as soon as possible.

Conclusion

The World Economic Forum Internship 2026 in Beijing is a highly competitive and prestigious opportunity that offers unparalleled exposure to global leadership, policy-making, and international collaboration. Whether you are interested in government relations, business strategy, or event experience, this programme provides a platform to develop your career while contributing to meaningful global change.

Discover more global Internship opportunities on OFY Here.

The post World Economic Forum Internship 2026 in Beijing: Government, Business & Event Experience Roles for Global Impact Careers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: World Food Forum Startup Innovation Awards 2026: Global Competition Offering Funding, Mentorship and International Exposure for Youth-Led Agrifood Startups
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
If you are a young innovator working to solve global food challenges, the World Food Forum Startup Innovation Awards 2026 is one of the most prestigious international opportunities you can apply for right now. Hosted under the World Food Forum and supported by the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations, this initiative is […]

The post World Food Forum Startup Innovation Awards 2026: Global Competition Offering Funding, Mentorship and International Exposure for Youth-Led Agrifood Startups appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are a young innovator working to solve global food challenges, the World Food Forum Startup Innovation Awards 2026 is one of the most prestigious international opportunities you can apply for right now. Hosted under the World Food Forum and supported by the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations, this initiative is designed to identify, fund, and scale groundbreaking solutions transforming agrifood systems worldwide.

What Is the World Food Forum Startup Innovation Awards?

The World Food Forum Startup Innovation Awards (SIA) is a global competition aimed at empowering young entrepreneurs who are developing innovative solutions to transform food systems. It focuses on solving urgent global challenges such as:

  • Food insecurity and hunger
  • Climate change impacts on agriculture
  • Food waste and inefficiency
  • Nutrition and equitable food access

The initiative aligns with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and seeks to accelerate impactful innovations through funding, mentorship, and global exposure.

Selected startups gain access to world-class accelerator programs, industry experts, investors, and international networks, helping them scale their solutions globally.

Categories of Innovation

Applicants can submit solutions across several high-impact categories within the agrifood ecosystem. These include:

  • Digital Innovation in Food Processing
  • Climate Resilience and Water Security
  • Food Security and Nutrition
  • Empowering Women in Agrifood Systems

Each category focuses on solving real-world challenges using technology, sustainability practices, and scalable business models.

Who Can Apply? (Eligibility Criteria)

This opportunity targets youth-led startups globally, making it highly inclusive but also competitive.

Eligible Applicants

  • Young entrepreneurs and startup founders
  • Early-stage agrifood startups
  • Teams working on innovative food system solutions

Age Requirement

  • At least one founder or senior executive must be between 18 and 35 years old

Startup Requirements

Applicants must meet the following conditions:

  • Must be an early-stage startup (pre-seed or seed stage)
  • Must have a validated solution (prototype, pilot, or early traction)
  • Must demonstrate scalability potential
  • Must address a clear agrifood challenge

Financial Criteria

  • Startups should typically have raised less than $2 million in funding

Eligible Countries and Regions

One of the strongest aspects of this opportunity is its global inclusivity.

  • Applicants can come from any country worldwide
  • Open to participants from Africa, Asia, Europe, North America, South America, and Oceania
  • Particularly encourages solutions that can scale across developing and underserved regions

This makes it an excellent opportunity for innovators from Africa, including Kenya, Nigeria, South Africa, and beyond.

Benefits of the Program

Winning or even participating in this competition comes with significant advantages:

Financial Support

  • Cash prizes of up to $15,000–$17,000

Mentorship and Training

  • Access to global experts and industry leaders
  • Participation in accelerator programs like MassChallenge Switzerland

Global Exposure

  • Opportunity to pitch at an international मंच in Rome
  • Visibility among investors, policymakers, and partners

Networking Opportunities

  • Connections with global innovators and institutions
  • Partnerships with leading organizations in agriculture and technology

Business Growth Support

  • Investor access
  • Scaling strategies and business development guidance

Application Timeline and Process

The selection process is rigorous and designed to identify high-impact innovations.

Key Stages

  1. Application Phase – Submit your startup details before the deadline
  2. Screening & Shortlisting – Evaluation by experts
  3. Semi-Final Stage – Additional assessments and refinement
  4. Finalist Selection – Top startups selected
  5. Final Pitch Event – Live pitching in Rome during WFF event

Why This Opportunity Matters

Global food systems are under increasing pressure due to climate change, population growth, and resource scarcity. Programs like the World Food Forum Startup Innovation Awards are critical because they:

  • Empower youth innovation
  • Accelerate scalable solutions
  • Bridge the gap between ideas and real-world impact
  • Support sustainable development globally

The program is not just a competition—it is a launchpad for global change-makers in agriculture and food systems.

How to Apply

To apply, visit the official application portal

Make sure your application clearly demonstrates:

  • The problem you are solving
  • Your innovation and how it works
  • Evidence of validation or traction
  • Your scalability plan
  • The impact your solution can create

Apply here

Deadline

9th May 2026

The World Food Forum Startup Innovation Awards 2026 is a high-impact global competition designed for ambitious young innovators ready to tackle some of the world’s most pressing food challenges. With funding, mentorship, and international exposure at stake, this opportunity can transform your startup from a local idea into a global solution.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post World Food Forum Startup Innovation Awards 2026: Global Competition Offering Funding, Mentorship and International Exposure for Youth-Led Agrifood Startups appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: The Conversation Prize for Writers 2026: Win £1,000, Mentorship, and Book Development Support for Your Research Writing
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for The Conversation Prize for Writers 2026, an exciting annual competition designed for academics and researchers who want to transform their expertise into compelling longform writing for a wider audience. Organized by The Conversation in partnership with Faber and The Curtis Brown Group, this prestigious competition offers researchers the opportunity to […]

The post The Conversation Prize for Writers 2026: Win £1,000, Mentorship, and Book Development Support for Your Research Writing appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for The Conversation Prize for Writers 2026, an exciting annual competition designed for academics and researchers who want to transform their expertise into compelling longform writing for a wider audience.

Organized by The Conversation in partnership with Faber and The Curtis Brown Group, this prestigious competition offers researchers the opportunity to publish their work, receive mentorship from publishing professionals, and potentially develop their ideas into a bestselling nonfiction book.

Whether you are sharing groundbreaking findings, reinterpreting established knowledge, or presenting a unique perspective from your field, this competition rewards exceptional writing that makes academic expertise accessible to a general audience.

What Winners Receive

The winner of the 2026 prize will receive:

  • £1,000 cash prize for the winning article
  • Online publication with The Conversation Insights
  • Mentorship from a book editor at Faber
  • Mentorship from a literary agent at Curtis Brown
  • Guidance to develop the submission into a nonfiction book proposal

This opportunity can significantly elevate the visibility of your research while opening doors to publishing and media opportunities.

Who Can Apply

The competition is open to academics and researchers interested in communicating their work to non-academic audiences.

Submissions must be written for general readers and fall within one of the following subject areas:

  • History
  • Arts and Culture
  • Business and Economy
  • Education
  • Environment
  • Health
  • Politics and Society
  • Science and Technology
  • World Affairs

Submission Requirements

Applicants must submit:

  • A 2,000-word article written for a non-academic audience
  • A book idea pitch of 350 words or less based on the article
  • A brief 100-word author bio explaining expertise and suitability
  • Disclosure of any conflicts of interest

Why This Competition Matters

Beyond the cash prize, The Conversation Prize is a unique platform for academics who want to:

  • Build a public profile for their research
  • Improve research communication skills
  • Explore traditional publishing opportunities
  • Connect with literary and publishing professionals

Deadline

All submissions must be received by:

Sunday, 5 July 2026 at 11:59 PM BST

How to Apply

Submit your application materials via email to :

uk-prize@theconversation.com

For further information about this opportunity , Visit here.

For more Award opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post The Conversation Prize for Writers 2026: Win £1,000, Mentorship, and Book Development Support for Your Research Writing appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Global Day of Action 2026: A Powerful Global Youth Climate Education Opportunity Driving Real-World Impact
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Global Day of Action (GDA) 2026 stands as one of the most impactful global youth-led climate initiatives, designed to empower young people to lead meaningful environmental change through education. Organized under QS ImpACT, this program is not just an event—it is a growing international movement mobilizing youth across continents to educate younger generations on […]

The post Global Day of Action 2026: A Powerful Global Youth Climate Education Opportunity Driving Real-World Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Global Day of Action (GDA) 2026 stands as one of the most impactful global youth-led climate initiatives, designed to empower young people to lead meaningful environmental change through education. Organized under QS ImpACT, this program is not just an event—it is a growing international movement mobilizing youth across continents to educate younger generations on climate change and sustainability.

Now entering its fourth edition, the Global Day of Action has already made significant global strides, reaching over 87 countries and engaging thousands of young leaders who are actively shaping climate awareness in their communities. The 2026 edition aims to expand this impact even further by focusing on a critical theme: Climate Education for the Next Generation.

This initiative recognizes a crucial truth: sustainable climate action begins with awareness, understanding, and education.

Why the Global Day of Action 2026 Matters

Climate change is no longer a distant threat—it is a present-day global crisis disproportionately affecting young people and future generations. Research from major international bodies highlights the urgency:

  • Around 1 billion children are at extremely high risk due to climate-related impacts.
  • Approximately 70% of young people report lacking sufficient knowledge to fully understand climate change.

The Global Day of Action directly addresses this gap by equipping youth with the tools, knowledge, and platform to educate others—especially children—about climate science, sustainability, and actionable solutions.

By participating, young leaders contribute to building:

  • Climate-literate communities
  • Resilient future generations
  • Locally driven environmental solutions
  • A globally connected network of changemakers

Overview of the Global Day of Action 2026 Program

The Global Day of Action is a structured yet flexible initiative that allows participants to create localized impact while being part of a global movement.

Core Theme for 2026:

Climate Education for the Next Generation

Through partnerships with organizations like Earth Quest, participants receive access to high-quality, youth-friendly educational materials designed to simplify complex climate topics for younger audiences.

Key Goals and Strategic Pillars for 2026

The 2026 Global Day of Action is guided by four major strategic goals:

1. Global Participation

  • Mobilize over 150 youth leaders and teams worldwide
  • Expand representation across diverse countries and communities

2. Climate Literacy

  • Educate more than 3,000 children and youth
  • Promote understanding of climate science, sustainability, and solutions

3. Capacity Building

  • Activate schools, youth councils, and community groups
  • Enable local workshops, awareness sessions, and training programs

4. Youth Leadership Development

  • Build a network of Climate Education Ambassadors
  • Encourage long-term engagement in environmental advocacy

What Participants Will Do

Participants in the Global Day of Action take on active leadership roles in their communities. Responsibilities include:

  • Teaching children about climate change using engaging and interactive resources
  • Organizing workshops, awareness campaigns, or educational sessions
  • Promoting sustainable habits and environmental responsibility
  • Documenting and sharing their impact through reports, photos, or testimonials
  • Collaborating with a global network of youth leaders

This is not a passive program—it requires initiative, creativity, and commitment to driving change at the grassroots level.

Step-by-Step Participation Process

The program is designed to be accessible and easy to follow:

Step 1: Sign Up

Participants register and receive a comprehensive Climate Education Starter Kit containing ready-to-use materials.

Step 2: Take Action

Host a climate education session in any format:

  • School-based workshops
  • Community events
  • Online webinars or campaigns

Step 3: Share Your Impact

Submit an impact report showcasing:

  • Activities conducted
  • Number of people reached
  • Visual or testimonial evidence

Apply here

Recognition and Benefits

Participants and top-performing initiatives gain significant recognition and opportunities:

  • Global feature on the QS ImpACT platform
  • Recognition at the QS ImpACT Youth Summit
  • Eligibility for sustainability awards and showcases
  • Access to a global network of youth changemakers
  • Enhanced leadership, communication, and project management skills

This recognition can be valuable for academic, professional, and leadership portfolios.

Who Can Apply?

Eligible Participants:

The Global Day of Action is open to a broad and inclusive audience:

  • Students (secondary school, college, and university)
  • Young professionals
  • Youth leaders and activists
  • Members of organizations, NGOs, or community groups

Geographic Eligibility:

  • Open to applicants from all countries worldwide
  • No restriction based on continent or region

Ideal Candidate Profile:

While there are no strict academic or professional requirements, ideal participants should:

  • Have a strong interest in climate change and sustainability
  • Demonstrate leadership or initiative
  • Be willing to engage with children or youth audiences
  • Possess basic communication and organizational skills
  • Be motivated to create real-world impact

Tools and Resources Provided

Participants receive a Climate Education Starter Kit, which includes:

  • Structured lesson plans
  • Interactive learning activities
  • Climate education materials tailored for younger audiences
  • Guidance on hosting effective sessions

This ensures that even individuals without prior teaching experience can successfully participate.

Global Impact So Far

The Global Day of Action has already demonstrated significant success:

2025 Highlights:

  • 645+ ambassadors
  • Representation from 60+ countries
  • Over 15,000 individuals educated

With each year, the initiative continues to grow in scale and impact, making 2026 its most ambitious edition yet.

Partnership Opportunities

Organizations can also engage with the Global Day of Action as partners. This includes:

  • NGOs
  • Universities
  • Corporations
  • Foundations

Benefits of Partnering:

  • Expand global reach across youth communities
  • Strengthen organizational commitment to sustainability
  • Support development of future climate leaders
  • Contribute to scalable climate education initiatives

Why You Should Apply

The Global Day of Action 2026 is more than a volunteer opportunity—it is a platform for leadership, global collaboration, and meaningful change.

By joining, you will:

  • Become part of a global climate movement
  • Gain hands-on leadership experience
  • Contribute to solving one of the world’s most urgent challenges
  • Inspire the next generation to take climate action

Deadline

6th May 2026

The Global Day of Action 2026 represents a unique opportunity for young people to move beyond awareness and take tangible action. In a world increasingly shaped by environmental challenges, initiatives like this play a critical role in empowering youth to lead solutions. If you are passionate about climate change, education, and community impact, this program offers the tools, platform, and global network to turn your passion into action.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Global Day of Action 2026: A Powerful Global Youth Climate Education Opportunity Driving Real-World Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
